ending: The final cell mission nearly destroys Tala's new flock. The imprisoned arcane ruler severs Terry from his body and then tears Tala's soul and gate free of hers. Rain's connection to Tala prevents her from passing on, while his berserker state resists the prisoner's soul attacks. Tala uses her ties to her family and her authority over the iron in her body to reclaim it, then reconstructs Terry around his preserved spirit. The entire unit survives and the Paragon closes the cell, but the ruler remains alive inside it. Rain proposes afterward, and Tala accepts. Terry survives as Tala's soulbound flockmate, while her inner collaborator withdraws to recover from the severing. Kit now shelters Lynn's expanding reborn community, Lisa's protected four-dimensional home, and the immortal elk's autonomous forest, but its magical wealth must remain guarded. Tala is close to Paragon and has accepted the enslaved identity imposed by the House of Blood as part of her history. Her next chosen reckoning is a voluntary return south to confront that house.
in_short: Tala develops her purpose as a protector while building Kit's pocket community, strengthening her magic, and deepening her relationship with Rain. Terry chooses an equal soulbond with her, gains fuller awareness, and commits to their new flock after confronting the loss of his first. Tala studies damaged reality, negotiates an autonomous home in Kit for an immortal elk, welcomes Lisa and a growing reborn population, and accepts the enslaved identity she had tried to separate from herself. During a final cell closure, an arcane ruler destroys Terry's body and tears Tala's soul from her own. Her bond with Rain keeps her from passing on; she reclaims her body through family and iron, restores Terry, and helps the unit survive until the cell closes. Rain then proposes, and Tala accepts. She remains short of Paragon and plans to face the southern house that enslaved her.

recaps.text: Previously: Tala became a powerful iron and void mage, a Defender, and the owner of Kit, a sentient dimensional refuge containing both her sanctum and a pocket town. Terry, a flicker-teleporting avian predator, became her close companion and protector. Simon, Petra, Adrill, Brandon, and Kedva formed part of the household and community within Kit. The refuge gave this group a protected place to live and work while Tala continued taking on dangers beyond it. Tala survived a near-fatal wolf encounter and responded by pursuing greater strength and broader defensive options. Rain reached Refined, bringing new physical and magical capacity that he still needed to master. After a period of tentative courtship, he and Tala stood ready to decide what their relationship would become. Tala also carried an internal co-operator who could advise her, comment on her personal choices, and share the work of managing their unusual life.

Rain joins Tala's Defender unit and begins gaining practical experience with his Refined body, inscriptions, and soulbound sword. He and Tala agree to date openly. Tala divides Kit into separately accessed sanctum and pocket-town partitions, increasing its usefulness while limiting direct movement between them. On cell duty, the unit confronts a fount that learns from each failed copy it creates, then survives false versions of its own members. The damaged fount is resealed rather than destroyed. In Kit, Kedva gives birth to a son, and Tala successfully performs magical rebirth on the infant with Holly's approved schema. The result establishes that a child can accept the transformation safely, provided dense magic remains available. Tala also develops new iron workings and considers stronger defensive tools. By reviewing why she pursues so many projects, she identifies a common aim: protecting and nurturing people so they can survive, grow, and choose what to become.

Tala conducts a broad review of her weaknesses and commissions improved armor, modular defensive walls, and a breath weapon built around an artificial lung inside Kit. She tests the weapon successfully but still needs safer control and containment. She relocates the pocket town to another city, opens it to trade, and allows gateless people to inquire about residence. There she reunites with Lynn and meets Lisa, a fox-like arcane specialist whose shop lies behind a four-dimensional entrance. Tala and Rain refuse to exploit the rule compelling Lisa to answer certain questions, so he instead shares basic knowledge voluntarily. Public respect for Tala's Defender work contributes slightly to her advancement. During a long meditation, Tala clarifies that her path is not merely to stop danger. She wants those under her protection to thrive and gain the means to fulfill their potential through freely chosen opportunity. Holly then removes Tala's old inscription metals so a stronger schema can be installed.

Holly rebuilds Tala's inscriptions, greatly improving her physical ability, perception, breath working, and control of magical leakage. Tala abandons a dangerous attempt to compress her own reality node and turns toward slower, safer research. She and Rain begin immersive war-game training in vulnerable mundane bodies, exposing habits concealed by their usual strength. Tala finalizes plans for flexible white-steel armor and modular fortifications. Kit's pocket town grows into a functioning community with its own access procedures and about fifty vetted residents. Tala and Rain travel with it to Marliweather, where Tala's brother chooses the academy. Tala's sister Nia challenges Tala's pattern of appearing mainly for academy decisions. Nia explains that glassmaking gives her meaningful work without magic, and Tala accepts that choice. Tala also explains that her attention comes from wanting her siblings to have options she once lacked. Their family begins arranging regular visits, while Tala and Rain confirm that any marriage or children remain private decisions for a much later time.

Tala reconnects with her siblings and completes the mundane phase of war-game training with Rain. They agree to travel together after the waning and share their first kiss. A cell assignment reveals that a soulless, regenerative former healer escaped long ago after lethal experiments on children. When Tala amplifies the remaining link, the fugitive projects herself into the cell, pressures Tala for answers, and offers to take and transform her. Greater outside powers drive the projection away, but Tala is disturbed by the comparison between their methods. Rain proposes an open-ended leave. Tala, Rain, and Terry travel to a ruined city where Tala can directly perceive wounded reality. Her own passage worsens the damage, while extradimensional creatures appear to consume excess magic around the rents. Tala rejects harming innocents for a supposed greater good and sharpens her protective principle. She then clashes with Cethira, a secret-bound three-gated mage. Cethira escapes and reports the encounter, while Tala concludes that damaged reality can heal and may someday be repaired deliberately.

Cethira is revealed as a survivor of forced experimentation that joined multiple souls or gates within her. She remains free but fears repeating the coercion that created her. Tala's group continues north, where older ruins show that reality's wounds can gradually close. Tala begins to see repairing the damage caused by magic as a possible long-term purpose. They then visit Terry's former nesting ground and find the remains of his mate and six hatchlings. After burying them, Terry defeats the local flock's leader but chooses to remain with Tala and Rain. He agrees to a deliberately shaped soulbond with Tala that preserves his identity and grants companionship, shared longevity, and greater awareness. The transformation forces him to confront his history, including mass violence after his first flock was killed and his gradual choice to protect Tala. Terry completes the bond by trusting Tala, becomes Refined, and gains shared flickering, healing, perception, and access to her power. The city can now recognize him through Tala's magical signature.

Tala and Terry spend months learning the combat and personal consequences of their completed bond, then return to cell work. Their unit finds an immortal, reforged elk confined with the ancient forest bound to him. Rather than reseal the cell in place, Tala proposes moving the whole region into Kit. She and Rain negotiate honestly with the elk, who refuses soulbinding and any obligation to defend humanity. He agrees to relocation in exchange for expansion of his forest, with additional space serving as payment for information or non-invasive study. Tala absorbs the cell successfully and grants the elk practical authority within his forest while retaining a final defensive claim through Kit. The elk explains distinctions among godbeasts and other great powers, and suggests that humanity's growing use of magic is accelerating the abrasion of reality and the failure of cells. Inspection finds the relocated forest secure. Rain also gives Terry a memorial representation of his lost mate, allowing Terry to grieve her presence as well as her absence.

Terry learns to move through Alefast under a silent human illusion and quietly visits people he knows. Holly creates an alternate interface for Rain, a new parallel consciousness that begins learning how to coexist with him and communicate through Tala's collaborator. Lisa inspects Kit's pocket town and discovers that its purifier left an immense reserve of magic unbound. He warns that the town's magical wealth, Tala's independence, and any rebirth program could attract hostile attention from the major arcane houses. The residents vote to admit him, and he agrees to build a protected four-dimensional home and help train future reborn residents under negotiated terms. Tala's unresolved trauma from the House of Blood remains severe. Advanced war games teach Tala and Rain to question apparent objectives and avoid assuming they understand another person's capabilities. Rain's new co-operator aids him during those trials and develops his own feelings about Tala. Family developments continue as Anna's brother graduates with oxidation magic and accepts Tala's advice to seek a suitable master rather than imitate her dangerous, exceptional path.

Lisa relocates his former shop into Kit, while Lynn retires from the Caravaners Guild and accepts a trial role administering the pocket town. Under her coordination, roughly one resident per day undergoes magical rebirth, and the new mages train in carefully managed dense magic. Anna becomes a master in Marliweather, and Tala continues supporting relatives as younger family members enter the academy or apprenticeships. Records of the Black Legion reveal the scale of the danger it poses, while worsening cells and damaged reality remain unresolved. Tala agrees to escort two siblings toward their masters in a former city, following the route tied to her earlier abduction. At the place where she was captured and later killed her captor, she confronts the identity imposed on her by the House of Blood. Tala accepts that this enslaved self and the mage she became are one person, without excusing the coercion or killings involved. The acceptance advances her sharply toward Paragon. She resolves to return voluntarily to the southern arcane city and confront the house that enslaved her.

Rain returns to the graves of four caravan members who died because his former refusal to fight left them exposed. He accepts responsibility and advances after releasing the burden he had carried. Tala and Rain reaffirm their love. As years pass, Tala struggles with remaining close to mortal siblings whose lives will be brief beside hers, but chooses continued family connection despite the eventual grief. A final cell closure becomes a disaster when an arcane ruler ambushes the unit. He destroys Terry's body by severing its link to his soul, then tears Tala's soul and gate completely from her body. Rain's exceptional connection to Tala keeps her from passing on, and his berserker state resists the same attack. Tala follows her family connections back to her living body and reclaims it through her authority over its iron. She then rebuilds Terry around the spirit preserved in their bond. Every unit member survives, and the Paragon closes the cell while the ruler remains alive. Afterward, Rain asks Tala to marry him, and she accepts.
